channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: King Charles addresses US Congress, calls for unity in "dangerous era"
item.description: King Charles III became only the second British monarch to address the US Congress, emphasizing the "truly unique" UK-US relationship and declaring America "lies at the heart of NATO" amid global tensions. He received 12 standing ovations during the historic speech marking America's 250th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guards seize wartime power, diminishing Supreme Leader's authority
item.description: Two months into the war with the US and Israel, Iran no longer has a single undisputed clerical arbiter at the pinnacle of power—a significant break from the Islamic Republic's structure that could be hardening Tehran's stance in talks with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Putin accuses Ukraine of intensifying attacks on Russian civilian infrastructure
item.description: President Vladimir Putin said Ukraine has stepped up attacks on civilian targets in Russia, citing a Ukrainian drone strike that caused a major fire at the Tuapse oil refinery on the Black Sea coast.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: US imposes sanctions on 35 entities for aiding Iran's sanctions evasion network
item.description: The US government escalated pressure on Tehran by sanctioning 35 individuals and entities involved in Iran's shadow banking sector, and threatened sanctions against Chinese "teapot" refineries paying tolls for Hormuz shipments.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: US and Iran clash at UN after Tehran appointed to nuclear non-proliferation role
item.description: The United States and Iran clashed at the United Nations over Tehran's nuclear program after Iran was selected as one of the vice presidents at a month-long conference reviewing the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: US Gasoline Prices Near Four-Year High as Iran War Disrupts Supply
item.description: Average US pump prices have reached their highest level in nearly four years following the US-Israel attack on Iran, reflecting the ongoing energy crisis. The American Automobile Association data shows sustained pressure on fuel costs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: BP Profit Doubles to $3.2 Billion on Iran War Oil Trading
item.description: British oil major BP reported first-quarter profit of $3.2 billion, beating expectations by 20% and marking its highest profit since 2023. The Iran war boosted oil trading results significantly.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Over 1,100 Ukrainians Accused of Betrayal, One in Five Are Minors
item.description: A Reuters investigation reveals that since Russia's full-scale invasion, more than 1,100 Ukrainians have been accused of betrayal through arson, terrorism or sabotage, with 20% being minors. The case of one teenager illustrates how children can be both perpetrator and victim.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Groups Petition US Trade Agency to Combat Forced Labor Imports
item.description: Pro-tariff and human rights groups asked the Trump administration to impose new import bans, duties and quotas targeting forced labor practices in China and other countries, pressing trading partners to adopt US-style enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Ukraine reveals details of Russia's new S-71K cruise missile
item.description: Ukrainian military intelligence disclosed specifications of Russia's new air-launched S-71K "Kovyor" cruise missile, including its design and foreign components. The revelation comes as Kyiv continues to expose Moscow's weapons supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: UAE plans to produce 5 million barrels per day after OPEC exit
item.description: UAE Energy Minister Suhail Al Mazrouei announced plans to produce 5 million barrels of oil daily after leaving OPEC and OPEC+, marking a dramatic shift in global energy dynamics as the Iran war disrupts Gulf oil markets.
item.dc:creator: Gulf Today

item.title: German defense minister says Ukraine partnership benefits Germany
item.description: German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius highlighted how Germany benefits from defense cooperation with Ukraine, noting Kyiv's rapid development of battlefield-tested technologies that enhance German military capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Deutsche Welle

item.title: UK borrowing costs hit highest level since 2008
item.description: UK government borrowing costs closed at their highest level since 2008, driven by economic uncertainty and fiscal concerns under Prime Minister Keir Starmer's government amid global market volatility from the Iran conflict.
item.dc:creator: The Telegraph

item.title: Armenia and Azerbaijan maintain high military spending despite peace deal
item.description: Despite agreeing to a provisional peace agreement last August, both Armenia and Azerbaijan continue to maintain some of the world's highest military spending as a percentage of GDP, reflecting an ongoing "peace-through-strength" approach.
item.dc:creator: Eurasianet

item.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Seize Wartime Power, Blunting Supreme Leader's Role
item.description: Two months into the war with the US and Israel, Iran no longer has a single undisputed clerical arbiter at the pinnacle of power, a break with the past that may be hardening Tehran's stance in negotiations with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: US Intelligence Studies How Iran Would React to Trump Declaring Victory
item.description: US spy agencies are examining scenarios where President Trump declares a unilateral victory in the two-month-old Iran war, which has killed thousands and become a political liability for the White House.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Trump Says Iran Wants US to Open Strait of Hormuz "As Soon As Possible"
item.description: President Trump stated that Iran has communicated it is in a "state of collapse" and wants the United States to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while it sorts out its leadership situation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: First Japan-linked Tanker Crosses Strait of Hormuz Since Iran War Began
item.description: The Panama-flagged tanker Idemitsu Maru, carrying 2 million barrels of Saudi oil, crossed the Strait of Hormuz, becoming the first Japan-linked crude tanker to do so since the Iran war began in February.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Purdue Pharma Reaches Criminal Settlement Over OxyContin Marketing
item.description: Purdue Pharma reached a criminal settlement with the Justice Department over its marketing of OxyContin, resolving long-running litigation over the opioid crisis that killed hundreds of thousands of Americans.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: Venezuela Raids Polymarket Office Over Maduro Election Prediction Market
item.description: Venezuelan authorities raided the offices of prediction market platform Polymarket after it hosted betting on whether Nicolas Maduro would be overthrown, arresting a soldier accused of sharing military intelligence.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: Former FBI Director Comey Indicted for Second Time
item.description: The US Justice Department has indicted former FBI Director James Comey for a second time, CNN reported, marking an extraordinary escalation in the Trump administration's pursuit of its perceived adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: UAE OPEC Exit Analysis: What It Means for Oil Markets
item.description: The UAE's decision to leave OPEC after 65 years reshapes the oil cartel's influence and comes amid the Iran war's disruption of Gulf energy supplies. Analysts assess implications for global oil markets.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Google Signs Classified AI Deal with Pentagon
item.description: Google has signed a classified artificial intelligence contract with the Pentagon, Information reported, deepening the technology giant's involvement in US defense projects amid intensifying competition with China.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Gulf Leaders Meet in Jeddah to Discuss Iran War
item.description: Gulf state leaders convened in Jeddah to coordinate their response to the Iran war and its impact on regional security and oil exports, as the Strait of Hormuz remains blockaded.
item.dc:creator: Wall Street Journal
